channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Meta After Killing the Metaverse: ‘Just Kidding’
item.description: There's a reason Meta can't let the metaverse die so easily.

item.title: Quantum Computers Will Tap Out Before Breaking Encryption, Theory Claims
item.description: A small mathematical revision to quantum mechanics could effectively limit the purported infinite capacities of quantum computers—if validated, that is.

item.title: Amazon acquires autonomous robotics startup Rivr
item.description: Amazon has acquired Rivr, a startup focused on autonomous robotics. Rivr is based in Zurich and was valued at $110 million in a funding round from August 2024, which both Amazon and its CEO's Bezos Expeditions participated in. Financial details of the acquisition were not disclosed. Rivr's robots have four legs and wheels that allow it to maneuver on stairs and other potentially uneven surfaces. The company just released its second generation of the robot. The purchase will likely further Amazon's capabilities for ever-faster and more efficient package deliveries. "This acquisition reflects ou...

item.title: What to Know About ‘JoJo’s Bizarre Adventure’ Before Starting ‘Steel Ball Run’
item.description: A newcomer’s guide to the seventh part of 'JoJo's Bizarre Adventure' and an answer to the eternal question of part‑skipping.

item.title: Sony’s WF-1000XM6 wireless earbuds are on sale for the first time
item.description: Sony’s WF-1000XM6 are the best noise-canceling wireless earbuds you can buy, but they’ve been hard to recommend over the last-gen model because of the price. While the older version is often on sale for $248, the update costs $329 — and has never before been marked down. That’s why this deal is worth paying attention […]

item.title: Employees had to restrain a dancing humanoid robot after it went wild at a California restaurant
item.description: A dancing robot in a Haidilao hot pot restaurant boogied down a little too hard.

item.title: No Nvidia, No AMD, No Intel, No ARM: Meta plans inference-led RISC-y future without friends as 1700w superchip emerges with 30 PFLOPs performance and half Terabyte (yes 512GB) HBM
item.description: Meta develops MTIA custom chips and a 1700W superchip to run GenAI inference efficiently without relying on mainstream silicon vendors.

item.title: Concerns Over Meta’s Smart Glasses Have Reached the U.S. Senate
item.description: Turns out you're not the only one who thinks adding facial recognition to smart glasses is a bad idea.

item.title: Test du Roborock F25 Ace Pro : j’ai pulvérisé de la mousse sur mon carrelage
item.description: Et voilà un aspirateur-balais laveur 3-en-1, c'est Roborock qui continue à enrichir sa gamme F25... ici, aec le Roborock F25 Ace Pro. On va surtout s'intéresser au fait qu'il pulvérise une mousse ultra-dense via sa technologie JetFoaming. En maintenant la gâchette, il mélange air et solution nettoyante et la projette par la buse frontale pour dissoudre taches et graisses avant aspiration... ça fait rêver, non ? Testons-ça. ...

item.title: This Translator Will Help You Parse Your Boss’s Mind-Numbing LinkedIn Speak
item.description: Turn that corpo gibberish into actionable communication, because someone has to actually run this place.

item.title: Meta Is Building an Encrypted Chatbot After AI Agents Went Rogue and Expose Sensitive Data
item.description: Late is better than never.

item.title: DoorDash will start paying gig workers for creating content to train AI models
item.description: DoorDash has launched a new option for its gig economy workers to earn some extra cash. The delivery service introduced Tasks, which it describes as "short activities Dashers can complete between deliveries or in their own time." It gives taking pictures of restaurant dishes or recording video of unscripted conversations in languages other than English as examples. These materials will be used to train artificial intelligence and robotics models. A representative from DoorDash told Bloomberg News that it will use Tasks content for evaluating its in-house AI models as well as those made by its ...

item.title: Google is reportedly testing a Gemini app for Mac
item.description: Google is testing a version of its Gemini app for macOS, Bloomberg reports. The app would bring the AI assistant to uncharted territory, and in more direct competition with OpenAI's ChatGPT and Anthropic's Claude, both of which offer standalone Mac apps.Gemini remains accessible through the web, and it sounds like the macOS app offers the same set of features, with the ability to respond to prompts, search the web and generate text, images and code. The major differentiator of the Mac app could be a feature called "Desktop Intelligence," which gives Gemini a new source of information and conte...

item.title: Washington state has no income tax. It just passed one for millionaires that could be a model for other states
item.description: The bill would tax earnings over $1 million at 9.9%, which could raise billions annually. Expect a big fight from the “wealth defense industry.” People who live and work in Washington state don’t currently pay any income tax. But in a few years, a small group of residents will be subject to one: Washington lawmakers recently passed a bill that would impose a 9.9% tax on income earned above $1 million, which goes into effect on January 1, 2028....

item.title: Amazon's preview Spring Sale is slashing up to 45% off some of my favorite robot vacuums — these are the 7 best deals
item.description: I'm a robot vacuum tester, and these are the models I'd recommend (plus some advice on what to avoid).

item.title: Marvel Beats Marvel in Quest for Most Watched Trailer
item.description: 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day' just bested not only 'Deadpool & Wolverine,' but 'Grand Theft Auto 6.'

item.title: Scientists Built Tiny, Portable Atomic Clocks—and Naturally, They’re Headed for Drones
item.description: This breakthrough in precision timing is about the size of your fingernail and only loses one second every 30,000 years.

item.title: Marc Andreessen is a philosophical zombie
item.description: I admit, this is an innovation I did not see coming: Silicon Valley has invented the philosophical zombie from the classic thought experiment "lol how crazy would it be if there were a philosophical zombie." Until recently, the philosophical zombie was a concept closely associated with Australian philosopher David Chalmers, who defines it as "someone […]...

item.title: Secure your Microsoft system or suffer the same fate as Stryker – US tells companies to secure corporate accounts
item.description: CISA says hackers are actively seeking targets and urges companies to bolster their defenses.

item.title: How to watch RB Leipzig vs. Hoffenheim online for free
item.description: How to watch Bundesliga for free. Watch RB Leipzig vs. Hoffenheim in the Bundesliga for free from anywhere in the world.

item.title: Is Reddit down? User-reported server error issue appears resolved
item.description: Reddit was inaccessible to some users Thursday, March 19. Here's everything we know so far.

item.title: The coolest game controller for your phone is $50 off
item.description: The MCON is the closest to a Swiss army knife that a controller can be. No, it doesn’t contain a can opener, but it makes up for it with a surprising array of features. Once you attach your MagSafe- or Pixelsnap-ready phone to the top plate, pressing a button along its top edge satisfyingly pops […]

item.title: Polymarket continues its partnership spree with a Major League Baseball deal
item.description: MLB is only the latest in a string of recently announced partnerships involving the prediction market.

item.title: NASA-Backed Startup to Try Ingenious Asteroid Mining Idea: Just Bag It
item.description: TransAstra has developed a plan to bag an asteroid and move it to a stable near-Earth orbit for processing.

item.title: AI is rewriting business in real time—and most leaders aren’t ready
item.description: Speaking with Fast Company at SXSW, experts share how business leaders can be more agile in the face of technological disruption. With AI capabilities doubling in a matter of months, agility is no longer a competitive advantage for business leaders—it’s now become a survival skill.
